description_long: This is a single-story log cabin-style house with a rectangular footprint and a prominent gable roof covered in weathered, snow-dusted shingles. The exterior walls are constructed from horizontal wooden logs, giving it a rustic appearance, and the base is set on a stone or concrete foundation. The front features a wooden porch with railings and steps leading up to a single wooden door, flanked by two small windows. The side elevations each have a single window, while the rear is windowless, emphasizing privacy and insulation. The roof extends over the porch, providing some shelter, and the overall structure suggests a cozy, compact living space. The house is designed for residential use and would fit well in suburban or rural neighborhoods.
description: A rustic, single-story log cabin house with a gable roof, wooden porch, and snow-dusted shingles. The structure features horizontal log walls, a front entry with steps, and small windows.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entry door, two windows, a wooden porch with railings, and steps leading up to the house.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the gable end of the roof, a single window, and the side extension of the porch and steps.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the plain back wall with no windows or doors, and the full span of the gable roof.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a single window and the continuation of the porch and railing, mirroring the other side.
attribute_entryway_design: Wooden door with steps leading to a covered porch, surrounded by railings and flanked by small windows.
